title: eriogonum umbellatum var. furcosum
intro: Eriogonum umbellatum var. furcosum, also known as the sulphur buckwheat, is a perennial plant species native to North America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to act as a purgative, diuretic, and antiseptic. The sulphur buckwheat offers several health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving digestion, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of Eriogonum umbellatum var. furcosum can be made into teas, infusions, or salves, and are often used to treat a variety of ailments, from skin irritations to gastrointestinal issues.
